info.name: DiscoveryCollection-BB-9218-OEX
info.description:
This collection covers library related operations including creating libraries and managing team members within them through API in response to a discovery \[BB-9218\] OEX: Discovery for Creating a Content Library and Assigning Admin Roles via API in Open edX from Salesforce.

**Base URL:** `{{base_url}}` **Authentication:** All requests require a JWT token passed as:

```
Authorization: JWT {{jwt_token}}

 ```

---

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `base_url` | The base url is extracted and put sepparatly for reusability purpose, it can be merged to each request urls if prefered. |
| `jwt_token` | During demonstration, the value of jwt_token is extracted from an active session in a browser. A combination of edx-jwt-cookie-header-payload and edx-jwt-cookie-signature is used to form a complete jwt_token by which requests will be allowed. |
| `library_id` | Except when creating a new library record, all other api calls involve a specific library to wich you may add a new team member, modify role of a team member or other tasks. The library_id is a provided here as a parameter for reusability perposes only. |
| `teammember` | When performing access management, we can use an existing user to demonstrate add a new team member, modify or remove team member. teammember is separatly provided as a setting for reusability purposes. |

request.description:
This is a PUT request and it is used to overwrite an existing piece of data. For instance, after you create an entity with a POST request, you may want to modify that later. You can do that using a PUT request. You typically identify the entity being updated by including an identifier in the URL (eg. `id=1`).

A successful PUT request typically returns a `200 OK`, `201 Created`, or `204 No Content` response code.
